div.head: 29. Telegram 4301 From the Mission to the United Nations to the Department of State, September 16, 1975, 2115Z.Source: National Archives, RG 59, Central Foreign Policy Files. Unclassified; Immediate. Repeated to the Mission to the EC in Brussels, the Mission to the U.N. in Geneva, the Delegate to Multilateral Trade Negotiations in Geneva, and the Mission to the OECD in Paris. Kissinger’s address is published in Department of State Bulletin, September 22, 1975, pp. 425–441. Moynihan offered a brief, positive assessment of the United Nations General Assembly Seventh Special Session.
div.p:
FM USMISSION USUN NEW YORK
TO SECSTATE WASHDC IMMEDIATE 2071
INFO USMISSION EC BRUSSELS
USMISSION GENEVA
USDEL MTN GENEVA
USMISSION OECD PARIS

1. ALTHOUGH THIS IS NOT A FULL ASSESSMENT OF THE 7TH SPECIAL SESSION, IT IS INDICATIVE THAT THE FINAL DOCUMENT INCORPORATES 15 OUT OF THE 19 MAJOR PROPOSALS CONTAINED IN SECRETARY KISSINGER’S STATEMENT. SPECIFICS INCLUDE DIRECT OR INDIRECT REFERENCE TO:
(B) NTB RULES TO PROVIDE SPECIAL CONSIDERATION FOR DEVELOPING COUNTRIES;
(E) ASSISTANCE TO LDCS IN ENTERING LONG TERM CAPITAL MARKETS;
(G) INTERNATIONAL CENTER FOR EXCHANGE OF TECHNICAL INFORMATION;
(I) IMPROVING PRODUCTIVITY AND COMPETITIVENESS OF NON-FOOD, AGRICULTURAL AND FORESTRY PRODUCTS.
(M) EXPANDING CAPACITY OF CONSULTATIVE GROUP FOR INTERNATIONAL AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH;

2. IN ADDITION TO THE ABOVE, THE SECRETARY’S SPEECH INCLUDED SEVERAL USG POLICY STATEMENTS AND/OR COMMITMENTS. BY ROUGH COUNT, 13 (OUT OF 17) OF THESE ARE COVERED BY GENERAL PRINCIPLES CONTAINED IN THE FINAL DOCUMENT OF THE SESSION. THE AREAS COVERED INCLUDE PARTICIPATION IN COMMODITY AGREEMENTS OR NEGOTIATIONS, IMF BUFFER STOCK FINANCING, GENERALIZED PREFERENCES, PARTICIPATION AND INCREASED FINANCIAL SUPPORT OF REGIONAL FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS AND OF IDA, AND INCREASED BILATERAL FOOD ASSISTANCE. MOYNIHAN
